questions.question: Human factors that often contribute to accidents have been classified into three broad categories:

choices.text: A final career goal takes one primary form.

questions.correctChoiceText: The intermediate objectives are a step taken to attain the goal.

There should be no firefighter risk when there is significant structural damage.

questions.correctChoiceText: There should be no firefighter risk when there is significant structural damage.

questions.question: Policies and procedures are most effective when they:

choices.text: have been developed by chief officers.
are considered dynamic and evolving documents.
have been developed by the legal staff.

questions.correctChoiceText: are considered dynamic and evolving documents.

questions.question: The risk management plan must be reviewed:
When giving media interviews, you should:

choices.text: trust reporters only if they assure you that what you tell them is off the record.
not attempt to answer questions when you don't know or are unsure.
be as frank and open as possible, even if it means divulging confidential information.

questions.correctChoiceText: not attempt to answer questions when you don't know or are unsure.

questions.question: The categories of arson are called:
If a fire does not have a fresh supply of oxygen, the fire slowly dies down to a decay/smoldering phase. This may occur before:

choices.text: or after a flashover has occurred.

questions.correctChoiceText: or after a flashover has occurred.

questions.question: Which is correct regarding shared group values?

choices.text: Differences are allowed and may conflict with organizational values.
There is a constant shifting of leaders to share the work load.
Values of the organization are reflected in the attitude of the group.
The values of the organization have little impact on the group values.

questions.correctChoiceText: Values of the organization are reflected in the attitude of the group.

questions.question: Ideally, problems should be solved:

choices.text: at the lowest possible level within the organization or those directly involved.
at the highest level within the organization.

questions.correctChoiceText: at the lowest possible level within the organization or those directly involved.

questions.question: Which is one component in the National Incident Management System?

choices.text: fault or blame for inconsistent actions.
whether the incident commander was competent.
disciplinary action for violations of policy and procedures.

questions.question: By evaluating the smoke, you are able to:

choices.text: tell the light grey smoke is an indication of rapid heat release.
tell fire is in the decay stage because the smoke is dark.
interpret no smoke showing during size-up as a sign for less caution when entering the building.
know where the fire is and where it is going.

questions.correctChoiceText: know where the fire is and where it is going.

questions.question: One of Layman's five-step size-up process is:

choices.text: delegate the task of scene control to law enforcement.
escort the owner through the building to verify where valuables are stored.
limit the number of fire personnel in the area.
shut down the street to avoid any traffic.

questions.correctChoiceText: limit the number of fire personnel in the area.

questions.question: In terms of monetary losses, risk is classified as:

give them extra time at work to study.
require them to complete extra work assignments to groom them for a new position.
encourage them to do your work for a shift or two.

questions.correctChoiceText: create a professional development plan for them.

ask their subordinates as a group to assist in the rating.

questions.question: The post incident analysis should be a(n) ___ assessment of the emergency incident.

Statement 2: Any member in a group may emerge as the leader. Statement 3: Leaders of an informal group are usually appointed.

choices.text: Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.
Statements 1 and 2 are false; statement 3 is true.
Statement 1 is true; statements 2 and 3 are false.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.

the burn adjacent to a ventilation source.

questions.correctChoiceText: measuring the depth and extent of charring.

questions.question: Human factors that often contribute to accidents have been classified into three borad categories:

Directs the tactical operations to meet the strategic goals

Responsible for the collection, evaluation, and use of information

questions.question: The most popular reason for arson is:
When giving media interviews, you should:

choices.text: trust reporters only if they assure you that what you tell them is off the record.
not attempt to answer question when you don't know or are unsure.

choices.text: The company officer assigns a more experienced member to work with a new member.
The company officer acts as a trusted and friendly advisor to the new member.
The company officer states their expectations positively and sets behavioral limitations.
The company officer guides the individual member through new activities.

questions.question: The action used for discipline, determining the cause of inappropriate behavior and correcting it is:

choices.text: The origin is usually located at the base of the U or V shape.
U shapes are more indicative of arson fires.

Both U and V shapes indicate the intensity of the fire.

questions.correctChoiceText: The origin is usually located at the base of the U or V shape.

questions.question: A step in the change process is:

choices.text: allowing the change to occur without controlling it.
identifying resistance, but allowing members to overcome it without intervention.
stating the need for change in vague terms.

questions.question: Which statement regarding Divisions and Groups is most correct?

choices.text: Divisions are used to describe geographical areas.
Groups are not equal to Divisions.
Divisions are identified by Roman numerals.
Groups are used to describe geographical areas.

questions.correctChoiceText: Divisions are used to describe geographical areas.

questions.question: The initial accident review process starts with the:

choices.text: It should occur immediately after the incident by the company officer.

copies should be provided ahead of time.

questions.correctChoiceText: copies should be provided ahead of time.

questions.question: Which is correct regarding performance evaluations?

choices.text: They should be tied to pay increases to provide adequate compliance and motivation.
They are subject to review and approval by the collective bargaining agreement.
They require both parties to agree in unison.
They take place informally on a daily basis and formally on predetermined schedules.

questions.correctChoiceText: They take place informally on a daily basis and formally on predetermined schedules.

questions.question: A classification of fire cause is:
With regard to determining the cause of a fire, which is true?

choices.text: If the fire appears to the fire officer to be the result of a malicious or negligent act, an investigation need not be called.
If each of the specific components of the fire tetrahedron is not specifically identified, the fire should be called undetermined.
The term, suspicious, is a legitimate fire cause classification.
If all investigative possibilities have been exhausted and the cause cannot be found to a reasonable degree of probability, the cause of the fire is considered undetermined.

questions.correctChoiceText: If all investigative possibilities have been exhausted and the cause cannot be found to a reasonable degree of probability, the cause of the fire is considered undetermined.

questions.question: The goal of fire pattern analysis is to:

choices.text: determine if the strategy and tactics were effective.

choices.text: Subjectivity is essential in personnel evaluations.
The responsibility for formal performance evaluation always rests with the Level I Fire Officer.
Employee performance evaluations are held on a specific schedule, usually annually.
Employee performance evaluations are always formally scheduled.

questions.correctChoiceText: Employee performance evaluations are held on a specific schedule, usually annually.

questions.question: Mastering current knowledge and skills while acquiring new skills required for a promotion is part of:

choices.text: Fire officers should supervise firefighters the same regardless of the task they are performing.
Firefighters only require direct supervision while operating at emergency scenes.
Supervision should be delegated to improve increased firefighter morale.
The higher the risk, the more direct supervision is required.

questions.correctChoiceText: The higher the risk, the more direct supervision is required.

questions.question: The role of an expert witness at a fire investigation proceeding is to:

choices.text: identify all possible causes and eliminate them systemically.
build on the assumptions of the defendant.
assist the judge and jury to understand the facts.

questions.correctChoiceText: assist the judge and jury to understand the facts.

questions.question: Which is correct regarding personnel evaluations?

choices.text: To maintain a high level of performance, the officer should not advise the individual when the evaluation will occur.
Evaluations must include definite identifiable criteria.
The evaluation should include tasks that the individual is not trained to perform.
To reduce paperwork, supportive documentation should be available if requested.

questions.correctChoiceText: Evaluations must include definite identifiable criteria.

questions.question: At an emergency scene, the incident safety officer has the authority to ___ or ___ any unsafe action that is occurring.

choices.text: be a minimum of three pages.
provide a clear mental picture of the situation.

questions.correctChoiceText: provide a clear mental picture of the situation.

questions.question: When giving a press release, you should:

choices.text: use technical terms to prove your knowledge.

questions.question: One of the purposes of performance evaluations is to:

choices.text: provide conflict resolution between the employee and the officer.
state subjective opinions about the employee.

questions.question: Before leaving the scene, you should:

choices.text: have the unit remain in service even if critical equipment is not available.
conduct a formal post incident analysis with their crew.
inspect the crews to ensure they are operationally ready.

questions.correctChoiceText: inspect the crews to ensure they are operationally ready.

questions.question: Which best describes coaching.

choices.text: A more experienced member is assigned to work with the new member.
The officer guides the individual member through new activities to accomplish a goal, reinforce behaviors or develop specific skills.
The officer acts as a trusted and friendly advisor to the member.
The officer states their expectations positively and sets limitations.

questions.correctChoiceText: The officer guides the individual member through new activities to accomplish a goal, reinforce behaviors or develop specific skills.

questions.question: A certified fire investigator should be called when there is a:

choices.text: determination that an accident caused the incident.
determination that nature caused the incident.

questions.question: Which is correct regarding fire investigation?

choices.text: The depth of char equates to proven scientific chart that identifies length of burning.
Saddle burns can quickly confirm an incendiary fire has occurred.
The area of the most damage should not be assumed to be the area of origin.
Begin with the area of least damage to the area of most damage.

questions.correctChoiceText: Begin with the area of least damage to the area of most damage.

questions.question: You should review all department injury, accident, and health exposure reports in order to:

choices.text: maintain records in accordance with federal, state, and local regulations.
identify unsafe acts and work conditions.

questions.correctChoiceText: identify unsafe acts and work conditions.

questions.question: The establishment of Divisions or Groups:

choices.text: allows the Incident Commander to communicate directly with multiple individual company officers.
often exceeds the capability of one officer to manage the entire operation.

questions.question: Effective personnel performance evaluations:

choices.text: have generally stated goals and objectives.

questions.question: The first objective of an origin and cause investigation is to:

choices.text: deduce how the ignition source and fuel came together.
determine the fuel that was first ignited.

Which best describes mentoring.

choices.text: The officer states higher expectations positively and sets behavioral limitations.
The company officer acts as an advisor to the member.
A rookie member is assigned to work with the new member.
The company officer guides the individual member through any new activities.

questions.correctChoiceText: The company officer acts as an advisor to the member.

questions.question: Which is a type of motivational theory that you should use?
Since firefighters cannot train on every subject, you should:

choices.text: ask the firefighters what they want to train on.
focus on high-risk activities that are infrequently performed.
train on at least two subjects each shift.
concentrate your time on mitigation efforts.

questions.correctChoiceText: focus on high-risk activities that are infrequently performed.

questions.question: If an incident involved multiple jurisdictions, the post incident critique should:

choices.text: involve a representative of each agency.
be conducted by the person with the highest rank.
identify the agency who performed the best.

questions.correctChoiceText: involve a representative of each agency.

questions.question: The first step in developing a budget is to:

choices.text: gather information about needs and projected costs.
find out what money is available.
call other agencies and use their budget figures.
submit a cost estimate to the city record for a cost breakdown.

questions.correctChoiceText: gather information about needs and projected costs.

questions.question: Post incident reviews should review all facts and actions to identify:

choices.text: who should be in charge of future incidents.

questions.question: Typical reports used to export data to track trends are:

choices.text: maintaining status of all incident resources.
requiring responders to report to receive assignments.
identifying resources with active assignments in the operations section of the Incident Command System.
establishing the priorities for the emergency incident.

questions.correctChoiceText: establishing the priorities for the emergency incident.

questions.question: One purpose of an employee evaluation is to:

choices.text: determine whether a person is functioning relative to the needs of the department.
determine whether the department standards are adequate.
allow the employee to have input into developing department standards.

questions.correctChoiceText: determine whether a person is functioning relative to the needs of the department.
